target.product_type: Desktop-first football scouting career simulation with responsive web UI
target.primary_user_goal: Plan scouting work, interpret uncertain evidence, recommend players, and build a career
target.primary_business_goal: Make the Youth Scout Early Access fantasy legible, trustworthy, and compelling enough to sustain long careers
target.audience: Football management-simulation players who want a scouting-centered career fantasy
assumptions: Youth Scout and Freelance are the only normally playable Early Access specialization and starting path.
Visual captures use the packaged build for a stable, reproducible render; final source typecheck and production build passed.
Unavailable career paths are excluded from visual scoring.

flows_walked: School Match live observation, insight, halftime, and reflection
Player dossier and persistent note review
Report writing, history, marketplace, and finance feedback
automated_checks: DOM inventory of headings, accessible names, font sizes, colors, and control dimensions
66 of 66 desktop planner scheduling controls and 68 of 68 mobile controls measured below 44px
Serious desktop color-contrast violations on dashboard and calendar
Moderate landmark, H1, region, and heading-order violations

visual_hierarchy.rationale: The main menu and onboarding have clear focal points, but the dashboard timeline and planner grids compete with the next scouting decision.
evidence: Large wordmark and single emerald primary CTA create a strong main-menu hierarchy.
Season timeline dominates the fresh dashboard before it creates an action.
Planner rows and tiny day buttons have nearly equal visual weight.

layout_spacing.rationale: The atmospheric shell and centered onboarding are controlled, while the planner wastes desktop space and compresses mobile content.
evidence: Main menu and identity card remain balanced at both captured viewports.
Mobile calendar clips activity labels and becomes a long repeated matrix.
Achievement toast competes with first-viewport mobile content.

typography.rationale: The type family and weight system feel cohesive, but operational labels shrink to 9-12px and semantic heading order is inconsistent.
evidence: Planner day buttons measured around 9px.
Identity onboarding lacks an H1 and planner content skips a heading level.
Raw internal perk IDs appear in player-facing career UI.

color_contrast.rationale: The emerald-on-charcoal palette is distinctive, but repeated muted text fails desktop contrast.
evidence: axe found serious color-contrast violations affecting six desktop dashboard nodes.
axe found serious color-contrast violations affecting six desktop calendar nodes.
Muted zinc 500/600 text is used for required operational metadata.

components_states.rationale: Cards, borders, and buttons share a visual language, but eligibility, affordability, and activity-specific states are inconsistent.
evidence: Insight actions costing 20-30 IP appeared enabled at 0 IP.
A Network Meeting exposed a Live Session affordance.
One selected focus target resolved and displayed as two.

interaction_feedback.rationale: The interface has motion, toasts, and summaries, but silent rejection and mismatched outcome values make feedback unreliable.
evidence: Unaffordable insight action closed without inline explanation.
Network feedback used generic scouting prose from a different activity.
Report craft and price differed across writer, history, and marketplace.

information_architecture_navigation.rationale: Progressive sidebar disclosure helps onboarding, but related player intelligence and history are split across overlapping screens.
evidence: Discoveries, Player Database, Youth Hub, and Alumni overlap as entity lists.
Observation Journal, Report History, finance, and outcomes do not form one case timeline.
Messages and metrics often lack source-entity deep links.

conversion_task_flow.rationale: Career setup is coherent and observation-to-reflection works, but planning and reporting impose excess clicks and omit stakes/context.
evidence: Planner requires targeting tiny individual day controls across repeated rows.
Report flow asks for conviction without an audience, brief, budget, or role need.
Targeted follow-up does not let the player select the target quality or hypothesis.

accessibility_inclusive_ux.rationale: Accessible names and some keyboard scaffolding exist, but contrast, semantics, target size, and mobile clipping are material failures.
evidence: Identity capture had moderate missing-main, missing-H1, and region violations.
Desktop dashboard and calendar had serious contrast violations.
All inventoried planner scheduling controls were below 44px; day buttons were about 17x16px.

imagery_iconography.rationale: Pitch imagery, green glow, silhouettes, and restrained line icons establish a strong scouting atmosphere.
evidence: Main-menu pitch image creates football context without competing with text.
Icon family is visually consistent and restrained.
Later dashboards rely more heavily on generic cards and lose some of the distinctive atmosphere.

brand_visual_system.rationale: The charcoal and emerald signal-in-darkness motif gives TalentScout a recognizable identity distinct from a generic sports dashboard.
evidence: Early Access and Youth Scout framing is visible at the primary CTA.
Color, imagery, and restrained tone support a serious professional fantasy.
Raw IDs and incorrect activity copy break brand professionalism during play.

emotional_trust_polish.rationale: The first impression and observation flow feel polished, while false achievements, clipped controls, and inconsistent outcomes erode trust.
evidence: Main menu feels unusually finished for an Early Access simulation.
Live observation and reflection create suspense and ownership.
Fresh-career achievement toasts and contradictory finance/report displays cheapen accomplishment and credibility.

conflicts: Polished visual controls do not consistently map to validated costs, targets, or committed outcomes.
Dashboards emphasize status and timelines over active scouting decisions and evidence gaps.
The desktop planner grid is compressed rather than redesigned for mobile.
Observation, report, stakeholder response, transaction, and outcome histories are fragmented.
strengths: Consistent charcoal, zinc, and emerald visual grammar.
Strong brand-emotion match for a serious football scouting fantasy.
Useful progressive navigation disclosure during early play.
caps_applied: Overall score reduced by 0.2 for interaction and journey cohesion conflicts.

overall.core_diagnosis: TalentScout has a memorable visual identity and strong first impression, but its working interface prioritizes dense status display and tiny repeated controls over the scout's next judgment, while cross-screen feedback inconsistencies undermine trust.

findings.summary: Planner control targets are far below practical touch and motor-accessibility guidance.
evidence: 66 of 66 desktop and 68 of 68 mobile scheduling controls were below 44px.
Day buttons measured about 17x16px with 9px labels.
findings.why_it_matters: Weekly planning is the central loop; difficult targeting turns strategic thought into motor friction and makes mobile play unreliable.

findings.summary: Required muted text fails contrast on desktop dashboard and calendar surfaces.
evidence: axe serious color-contrast violations affected six nodes on each captured desktop game state.
findings.why_it_matters: Costs, dates, status, and explanatory metadata are necessary for informed decisions, not optional decoration.

findings.summary: Control state and committed behavior disagree.
evidence: 0-IP insight actions appeared enabled and failed silently.
One selected prospect resolved as two targets.
Report values changed across three adjacent surfaces.
findings.why_it_matters: A simulation interface must teach causal rules; inconsistent feedback makes every later choice feel arbitrary.

findings.summary: The central scouting story is split across overlapping lists and disconnected histories.
evidence: Discoveries, Database, Youth, Alumni, Journal, Reports, finance, and outcomes require separate navigation.
findings.why_it_matters: Attachment, comparison, hindsight, and professional accountability require one persistent player/case context.

findings.summary: The report flow requests conviction without establishing the professional decision context.
evidence: No audience, club need, intended role, budget, wage context, deadline, alternative, or next action was required.
findings.why_it_matters: Without a brief, the interface rewards generic player quality instead of scouting judgment and club fit.

findings.summary: The planner's desktop matrix is compressed onto mobile rather than transformed into an agenda flow.
evidence: Mobile labels truncate, the page becomes very long, and achievement overlay consumes the first viewport.
findings.why_it_matters: Responsive usability requires changing the interaction model, not only shrinking the same grid.

findings.summary: Shared-looking activity cards expose actions that do not belong to their activity type.
evidence: Network Meeting exposed Live Session and returned Cast Wide Net copy.
findings.why_it_matters: Visual consistency is harmful when it implies behavioral consistency that does not exist.

findings.summary: Onboarding and planner heading semantics are incomplete.
evidence: Identity state lacked an H1/main landmark and calendar skipped heading levels.
findings.why_it_matters: Semantic hierarchy supports screen-reader navigation and reinforces visible hierarchy for all users.

findings.summary: Achievements and outcome displays can celebrate or report events the current career did not earn or cannot audit.
evidence: Fresh-career achievement toasts appeared during setup and obscured dashboard content.
Balance rose while the finance history reported no income.
findings.why_it_matters: Unreliable celebration cheapens pride and directly contradicts the product fantasy of earned professional reputation.

recommendations.title: Make the dashboard a decision agenda
recommendations.problem: The dashboard emphasizes season timeline and totals over urgent scouting judgments.
recommendations.expert_principle: Visual emphasis should match the user's immediate goal and expose progressive complexity.
recommendations.recommended_change: Lead with deadlines, active cases, evidence gaps, obligations, competitive heat, and cash risk; move background world/status lower.
acceptance_criteria: Every first-viewport card has a valid action or source link.
Urgent decisions visually outrank historical totals.
A player can identify the next three decisions in under ten seconds.

recommendations.title: Replace the planner micro-button matrix
recommendations.problem: Tiny repeated day controls create accessibility and high-click task friction.
recommendations.expert_principle: Fitts's Law, target clarity, and responsive task design.
recommendations.recommended_change: Use an opportunity board and calendar with large targets, schedule-best-slot, drag/drop plus keyboard alternative, and a mobile agenda/slot picker.
acceptance_criteria: Primary touch targets are at least 44x44px or meet spacing equivalence.
No clipped activity label at 390px.
A representative week can be scheduled in under 90 seconds by mouse, touch, or keyboard.

recommendations.title: Create one Recommendation Case workspace
recommendations.problem: Evidence, report, stakeholder response, transaction, and outcome history are fragmented.
recommendations.expert_principle: Entity continuity, recognition over recall, and narrative coherence.
recommendations.recommended_change: Unify brief, evidence, hypotheses, report versions, decisions, relationships, and long-term outcomes on one linked case page.
acceptance_criteria: Every report and outcome links to its case.
A player can reconstruct why the recommendation changed without opening another history screen.
Related player, club, contact, and finance entities deep-link bidirectionally.

recommendations.title: Bind every control to a validated command preview
recommendations.problem: Affordability, target counts, activity actions, and committed values disagree.
recommendations.expert_principle: Honest design, visibility of system status, and error prevention.
recommendations.recommended_change: Render exact cost, target, eligibility, and consequence category from the same validator/evaluation object that commits the action.
acceptance_criteria: Invalid controls are disabled with a readable reason.
Rejected actions preserve context and input.
Preview target/value metadata matches committed state.

recommendations.title: Establish accessible semantic and contrast tokens
recommendations.problem: Contrast, landmarks, heading order, focus, and small text failures repeat across screens.
recommendations.expert_principle: WCAG-oriented inclusive UX and systematic design tokens.
recommendations.recommended_change: Define measured text/state tokens, semantic page shells, logical headings, visible focus, and an operational typography floor.
acceptance_criteria: Zero serious or critical axe violations on critical routes.
Every route has one main landmark and H1.
Required text meets WCAG AA on its real surface and state is never color-only.

recommendations.title: Turn notifications into action and history
recommendations.problem: Toasts obscure mobile content and inbox volume substitutes for durable context.
recommendations.recommended_change: Queue non-blocking achievements, create a validated Action Inbox, and move background outcome detail into the relevant case timeline.
acceptance_criteria: Toast never blocks the primary action or core status.
Every action-required item has a valid command and deadline.
Background updates group and remain discoverable in entity history.

redesign_thesis: Turn TalentScout from a collection of dark management dashboards into a scout's evidence-and-decisions workspace: one prioritized weekly agenda, one persistent Recommendation Case for each consequential judgment, large validated actions, and explicit cause-and-consequence feedback.
acceptance_criteria: All critical routes have one main landmark, one H1, and logical heading order.
No serious or critical axe violations at desktop or mobile viewports.
No primary/touch scheduling control is an unspaced 17x16px target and no actionable label is 9px.
No clipped label or horizontal overflow at 390, 768, 1024, and 1440px.
Every ineligible action is disabled with a readable reason and preserves context on rejection.
Report preview, committed artifact, and marketplace base metadata derive from one evaluation object.
A player can navigate message to case to evidence/report to transaction/outcome and back without losing context.
Keyboard-only users can complete onboarding, schedule, observe, reflect, report, resolve inbox actions, and save/load.
